title: Chinese university builds 3D chip design tool tailored to Huawei's LogicFolding architecture 3D design delivers increased performance and better thermal management
summary: A Chinese university has developed a 3D chip design tool specifically for Huawei's LogicFolding architecture, enhancing performance and thermal management.
insight: Improved performance through 3D chip design and better thermal management.

title: Wide-ranging 7-zip vulnerability with 8.8 CVE rating allows for code execution hundreds of millions of machines potentially at risk
summary: A critical 7-zip vulnerability allows code execution with an 8.8 CVE rating, affecting hundreds of millions of machines worldwide.
insight: Massive user base makes widespread exploitation extremely concerning and feasible.

title: Samsung Display announces world's first 360 Hz 4K QD-OLED panel dual-mode support also offers 680Hz at FHD, 10 customers reportedly lined up
summary: Samsung Display announces 360 Hz 4K QD-OLED panel, a major gaming breakthrough. This panel also supports 680Hz at FHD. Several clients are reportedly lined up to use the technology.
insight: Revolutionizes gaming with ultra-high refresh rates and 4K resolution

title: Sandisk brings back affordable storage to rescue buyers from the SSD crisis new 320 and 520 SATA SSDs are ready to launch
summary: SanDisk is set to launch new SATA SSDs, the 520 and 320, with capacities up to 4TB, offering more affordable storage options for consumers.
insight: Affordable storage revival in midst of SSD price crisis.

title: Get 32GB of Corsair Vengeance DDR5 RAM for $240 when paired with this Asus motherboard start your AM5 PC for just $639, saving $245 in the process
summary: Get 32GB of DDR5 RAM for $240 with an Asus motherboard bundle. This affordable bundle starts your AM5 PC for $639, saving $245. Bundle includes Vengeance RAM and an Asus ROG Strix X870E-E motherboard.
insight: Save $245 with a RAM and motherboard bundle purchase.

title: ASRock BC-250 used for Steam Machine duty gains third-party hack to unlock all 40 CUs mining board now has more CUs than a base PS5
summary: ASRock's BC-250 server board has been hacked to unlock all 40 CUs, surpassing the base PS5's 36 CUs. This modification enables the mining board to utilize more processing power. The hack opens up new possibilities for gaming and cryptocurrency mining.
insight: Unlocking 40 CUs boosts processing power over base PS5.

title: AI costs begin to bite as agents may increase token demand by 24 times, says Goldman Sachs report Uber and Microsoft among companies feeling the bite of tokenized billing
summary: Rising token costs from AI agents threaten the viability of tokenized billing for tech companies, prompting a reevaluation of their AI approaches.
insight: AI agents drive 24x increase in token demand, crippling tokenized billing cost-efficiency.

title: Valve hikes Steam Deck OLED prices 512GB is now $789, while 1TB climbs to $949
summary: Valve's Steam Deck prices have increased by up to $300 due to component shortages.
insight: Component shortages drive significant price hikes for gaming devices

title: Italian council sets 200% tax on data center development in agricultural zones aims to spur the use of old industrial areas instead and limit environmental impact
summary: Italy introduces a 200% tax on data centers in rural zones, promoting development in disused industrial areas to reduce environmental impact.
insight: Reducing rural data centers' environmental footprint through industrial area repurposing.

title: Get your hands on a 2TB Samsung 990 Pro SSD for under $390 $250 savings brings one of the fastest PCIe 4.0 SSDs to its lowest price in months
summary: Samsung's 990 Pro 2TB is a high-performance PCIe 4.0 SSD with excellent efficiency and a 5-year warranty, offering a well-rounded storage upgrade. It delivers fast speeds and efficient use of resources for any build. At $389.99, it's a worthwhile investment for those seeking top-notch storage.
insight: High-speed storage upgrade at an affordable price point

title: Nvidia offers restricted access to Vera CPU in first round of Linux benchmarks - 88-core monster competes with or beats Epyc and Xeon in selected tests
summary: Nvidia's first-generation custom server core, Vera, performs closely to AMD's EPYC in benchmarks. Although it doesn't win outright in most tests, Vera's performance is impressive for a first-gen CPU. This indicates Nvidia's potential in the server CPU market.
insight: Nvidia's server CPU debut shows remarkable performance potential.

title: Erin Brockovich starts tracking AI data centers, calls on affected communities to submit issues website shows more than 2,700 reports from across the US raising various concerns
summary: Erin Brockovich tracks data center impact on communities, gathers reports nationwide.
insight: Data centers pose environmental and health risks to surrounding communities.

title: China adds homegrown AI chips to 'secure and reliable' procurement list for the first time nine options added as move away from Nvidia continues
summary: China has added nine homegrown AI chips to its secure procurement list, marking a shift away from Nvidia. The certifications are valid for three years, issued by the China Information Technology Security Evaluation Centre and the National Secrecy Science and Technology Evaluation Centre.
insight: China reduces dependence on foreign tech with domestic AI chip certifications

title: Veteran programmer finishes retro game sequel and publishes it after 37 years Colin Porch started Head Over Heels home computer title in 1989, but it was shelved due to console pivot
summary: Veteran programmer Colin Porch completes sequel to 1980s isometric puzzler Head Over Heels after 37 years. The original game was shelved in 1989 due to a console pivot. The sequel has now been published.
insight: Legacy projects can be revived with passion and dedication.

title: Prototype of the world s first fluid circuit board can be physically rewired in less than a minute, startup claims could make hardware iteration 1,000 times faster than traditional PCB
summary: A deep tech startup has created a fluid circuit board prototype that can be rewired in under a minute, potentially revolutionizing hardware iteration speeds.
insight: Accelerating hardware development 1,000 times faster than traditional PCBs.

title: Gesture HW1 is a 10-DOF ESP32-S3 robotic hand with high-dexterity manipulation (Crowdfunding)
summary: The Gesture HW1 is a 10-DOF robotic hand with ESP32-S3 MCU, designed for research, education, and hobbyists. It offers high-dexterity manipulation, weighs 500g, and can handle 1kg dynamic and 3kg static loads. It communicates via USB-C or Bluetooth 5.0 with a companion desktop app and SDKs.
insight: Revolutionizing robotics research and education with affordable high-dexterity manipulation.

title: Privacy-focused, open-source Raspberry Pi Zero 2W DIY security camera offers end-to-end encryption, on-device AI
summary: Secluso is a private, open-source security camera system built on Raspberry Pi Zero 2 W, offering end-to-end encryption and on-device AI for human, pet, and vehicle detection, providing a secure alternative to commercial smart home cameras.
insight: Private, end-to-end encryption secures DIY home security camera feeds locally.

title: VIA Labs VL610/VL610D MST Hub controllers to enable USB-C multi-display adapters with up to three 4K display outputs
summary: VIA Labs VL610/VL610D MST Hub controllers enable USB-C adapters to drive up to three 4K UHD displays simultaneously. The VL610 supports three video outputs up to 4Kp60, while the VL610D is limited to two outputs. These chips succeed the VL605 single-channel USB-C to HDMI 2.1 signal converter.
insight: Enabling multi-display expansion for USB-C adapters and docking stations.

title: Broadcom BCM68850 and BCM55050 SoCs target Wi-Fi 8 and 50G PON fiber gateways
summary: Broadcom has introduced two SoCs for 50G PON gateways with Wi-Fi 8 support, targeting ultra-fast connectivity for AI workloads and UHD streaming. The BCM68850 gateway SoC and BCM55050 Optical Network Unit (ONU) SoC deliver high-speed routing, Ethernet switching, and AI-driven functions. They are designed for end-to-end 50G fiber deployments with high throughput and low latency.
insight: Accelerating AI-driven home networks with 50G fiber deployments

title: Modos Flow An FPGA-based 13.3-inch USB-C touchscreen e-paper monitor (Crowdfunding)
summary: Modos Flow is a 13.3-inch e-paper monitor with a touchscreen and FPGA, featuring a 4096-color display, frontlight, and USB-C connectivity.
insight: Innovative e-paper display technology for eco-friendly and energy-efficient monitor options.

title: Microsoft is rolling out its Windows 11 CPU-boosting update here's how to enable it now
summary: Microsoft is rolling out a Windows 11 update that boosts CPU performance, accelerating app launches and core shell experiences. Update KB5089573 utilizes the LLP system to temporarily increase CPU clocks. This improvement aims to enhance overall system responsiveness.
insight: Temporary CPU clock boosts enhance Windows 11 system responsiveness

title: The Nvidia App Settings You Should Actually Be Using
summary: The Nvidia App offers a range of advanced features beyond driver updates, including monitor settings, DLSS overrides, and recording tools to enhance the gaming experience. These features can be customized to improve performance, graphics quality, and overall gaming experience. By utilizing these settings, PC gamers can unlock new capabilities and optimize their gaming setup.
insight: Unlocking advanced Nvidia features enhances gaming performance and visuals.

title: Steam Deck OLED is back in stock with a $300 price hike
summary: Steam Deck OLED prices have increased by $240-$300, with the LCD model discontinued.
insight: Steam Deck pricing shift affects affordability for PC gaming enthusiasts worldwide.

title: The Netherlands just blocked a US company from buying the app Dutch citizens use for everything
summary: The Netherlands blocked a US company's acquisition of a domestic app, showing a growing trend among European countries to protect their digital sovereignty and limit foreign influence on local service providers.
insight: European nations prioritize digital self-reliance over foreign investment and control.

title: Gigabyte bundles 1 gram of pure gold with every RTX 5090 Infinity to celebrate 40th anniversary
summary: Gigabyte is offering 1 gram of 999 pure gold with every RTX 5090 Infinity purchase. The promotion runs from May 25 to June 7. Eligible buyers will receive the gold item while supplies last.
insight: Gigabyte adds luxury to high-end graphics card purchases.

title: Taiwan just busted an alleged Nvidia GPU smuggling ring using Japan as a cover
summary: Taiwanese prosecutors detained three individuals for allegedly smuggling Nvidia GPUs using Japan as a cover. The investigation found falsified export documents for Super Micro Computer servers. At least one shipment of servers is believed to have slipped through.
insight: Nvidia GPU smuggling undermines global semiconductor export regulations tightly.

title: This startup wants to destroy cancer tumors using ultrasound instead of surgery
summary: HistoSonics is developing histotripsy, an ultrasound technique to destroy cancer tumors without surgery.
insight: Non-invasive cancer treatment could reduce patient trauma and recovery time.

title: TCL is selling a 300Hz 1080p gaming monitor for $88
summary: A 24.5-inch 1080p gaming monitor from TCL offers a 300Hz refresh rate for esports players prioritizing frame rate stability and low latency.
insight: Lower resolutions prioritize frame rate stability in competitive gaming scenarios.

title: Spotify CEO defends AI music, promises musicians will be adequately compensated
summary: Spotify CEO defends AI music, calling it a 'legal' and 'controlled' feature.
insight: Musicians' compensation is key to AI-generated content adoption success.

title: Ferrari bet its legacy on an EV designed by Jony Ive. The backlash wiped out $5 billion in a day.
summary: Ferrari's EV, designed by Jony Ive, received harsh criticism from Italy's deputy prime minister and others, deviating from Ferrari's established identity.
insight: Brand identity and legacy matter in electric vehicle innovation.

title: Apple's M5 MacBook Air drops to $899 in latest deal
summary: Apple's M5 MacBook Air drops to $899 with 16GB RAM and 512GB SSD.
insight: Affordable high-performance laptops boost consumer purchasing power.

title: DuckDuckGo's app is growing in popularity after Google turned search into AI
summary: Google's AI-driven search change is causing users to seek alternatives like DuckDuckGo.
insight: Users are seeking alternatives to avoid reinforcing AI-centric search.

title: Smartphone shipments face worst decline ever: memory crisis and US-Iran war hit market
summary: Global smartphone shipments are forecast to fall 13.9% in 2026 to 1.09 billion units, the lowest since 2013.
insight: Memory crisis and US-Iran war exacerbate struggling smartphone market.

title: Samsung averts strike with $340,000 bonuses for semiconductor workers
summary: Samsung averts strike by offering $340,000 bonuses to semiconductor workers, preventing industry disruptions.
insight: Semiconductor shortages have far-reaching impacts on multiple industries and economies.

title: Most Americans want digital shelf labels and surveillance pricing banned
summary: A survey found 65% of Americans believe ESLs will increase prices, and 68% think surveillance pricing will have the same effect.
insight: Concerns over price increases drive opposition to ESLs and surveillance pricing.

title: Inside the data center boom that's consuming Chile's last water
summary: Data centers in Chile consume billions of liters of water annually, exacerbating drought conditions in the Quilicura wetland.
insight: Water-intensive data centers threaten Chile's ecosystem and drinking water supply.

title: The Witcher 3 is getting a third expansion in 2027, but minimum specs are increasing
summary: A third expansion for The Witcher 3 is reportedly in development by Fool's Theory, the studio behind the first game's remake. The expansion is expected to be released in 2027. System requirements for the game may increase with the expansion.
insight: Future-proofing for The Witcher 3 requires updated hardware and specs.
